commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow code/test repairs in the Codex binding guard and command tests, with no new config surface or product decision needed.; Needs attention: The PR is security-relevant and still leaves a concrete legacy-binding path where node exec-host policy may not be enforced.; Review finding: [P1] Guard bindings that lack agentId — extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts:181 - [P2] Upd... (sha=4a6f0fd7f30c9542f3a30acf0b3384c237c63196)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional Codex app-server binding agent id, passes it into the native execution guard, and adds a regression test for bound app-server turns under an agent-level `tools.exec.host=node` policy.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +53. Total +69 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high-confidence: use an app-server conversation binding with no stored `agentId`, no inbound session key, and a non-default bound agent configured with `tools.exec.host=node`; the guard passes no agent id and the policy falls back to another agent. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow code/test repairs in the Codex binding guard and command tests, with no new config surface or product decision needed.

target.title: fix(codex): honor bound agent exec host policy
target.body:
## Summary
- store the binding agent id for Codex app-server conversation bindings
- pass the binding agent id into the Codex native execution guard
- block bound app-server turns when that agent resolves to `tools.exec.host=node`, even without a session key

## Verification
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
target.author: jesse-merhi
